g.text: SIGPIPE: Write to Closed Connection — Problem vs Fix
④ Server calls send() with check
tcp_state | TCP_FIN | FIN received from peer
snd_buf | bytes | kernel send buffer (still has space)
rcv_buf | 0 bytes | peer closed — RST will come
sk_err | EPIPE | error code set by TCP stack
signal(SIG_IGN) | process-wide | POSIX all | YES — servers
MSG_NOSIGNAL flag | per-call | Linux only | YES — portably
SO_NOSIGPIPE opt | per-socket | macOS/BSD | macOS fallback
SIGPIPE handler | process-wide | POSIX all | NO — re-raises
TCP FIN sent — client side closed
TCP RST received from peer — EPIPE condition
Signal sent to server process — default action: TERMINATE
TCP FIN sent — client side closed

text.tspan: // Then plain send() is safe on macOS
// Then plain send() is safe on macOS
